content:
Imagine you have a special box where you keep your money, and it's magic! Every year, the box gets a little bigger because it adds some extra money to what you already have. This is kind of like what happens when you put your money in a savings account that earns something called interest. 

At first, the extra money might seem small, but here's the really cool thing about it: the box doesn't just add extra money to what you first put in, it also adds extra money to the extra money it added before! This is called compound interest. Over time, your savings can grow a lot because the box keeps adding more and more. For example, if you start with $100 and it earns 5% interest each year, after one year you'll have $105, and after two years, you'll have a bit more than $110. It keeps growing like that, and it can add up to a lot of money after many years!
